Harry Potter is famous for being the chosen one, the Boy Who Lived, and the hero of the wizarding world. He is known for his bravery, his loyalty to his friends, and his determination to defeat the dark wizard Lord Voldemort. JK Rowling is a British novelist, screenwriter, and philanthropist best known for writing the Harry Potter fantasy series. The books have been translated into over 80 languages and have sold more than 500 million copies worldwide, making Rowling one of the best-selling authors of all time.
Early Life and Career
Joanne Kathleen Rowling was born on July 31, 1965, in Yate, Gloucestershire, England. Her mother, Anne, was a homemaker, and her father, Peter, was an engineer.
